meta.methodology: Classified each actual R1 pick from 2024 and 2025 into one of six buckets (PREDICTABLE, VISIT_SIGNAL, NEED_DRIVEN, SCHEME_FIT, TRADE_CASCADE, WILDCARD) by comparing the actual selection against contemporaneous NFLMockDraftDatabase consensus rank, PFF pre-draft grade, publicly reported top-30 visits, analyst-stated team needs, and documented trade activity at that slot. Each pick is then mapped to the 2026 model feature (board_rank, visit_count, roster_needs, scheme_tags, trade_cascade) that would have been required to catch it.
sources: NFLMockDraftDatabase consensus big boards archived April 2024 and April 2025
Wikipedia 2024 NFL Draft and 2025 NFL Draft pages for trades and actual picks
ESPN, The Athletic, Pro Football Network top-30 visit trackers

2024.note: slot 24 counted under TRADE_CASCADE via slot 23 move

feature_coverage.board_rank: 42% of R1 picks caught purely by consensus rank alignment (PREDICTABLE bucket)
feature_coverage.visit_count: ~78% of all R1 picks had a confirmed top-30 visit with the drafting team, but visit alone never overrode consensus enough to move slot — VISIT_SIGNAL as a pure-lift bucket was 0. Visit is a necessary filter, not a sufficient predictor.
feature_coverage.roster_needs: 19% of picks were primarily need-driven against consensus; combined with PREDICTABLE (which also leverages need), roster_needs is load-bearing for ~61% of picks
feature_coverage.scheme_tags: 14% of picks were pure scheme/archetype reaches (Mims, Chop, Morgan, Guyton in 2024; Loveland, Mykel Williams, Stewart, Zabel, Pearce in 2025). Our hybrid_flex + scheme_archetype_tags catches these ONLY if we have correct coach-archetype mapping.
feature_coverage.trade_cascade: 14% of R1 picks were re-priced by an intra-R1 trade. OUR WEAKNESS: we trigger trades probabilistically but do not cascade the pick distribution downstream — when KC trades up to 28, every pick from 28-32 shifts. Current model treats post-trade slots as independent.
feature_coverage.wildcard: 11% ceiling — Penix-to-ATL, Bo Nix R1, Bowers to LV, Pearsall to SF, Egbuka to TB, Hairston to BUF are genuinely unpredictable from public info. Hard cap on exact-slot accuracy.

systematic_misses_our_model_makes: TRADE_CASCADE under-propagation: 9 of 64 picks (14%) were reshaped by trades, but our simulator does not re-weight downstream team boards after a trade fires. This compounds error across 3-4 subsequent slots, not just the trading slot.

SCHEME_FIT under-indexed on traits-over-tape EDGE/OL reaches: Shemar Stewart (0 college sacks, R1), Chop Robinson (undersized hybrid), Amarius Mims (8 college starts), Tyler Guyton. Our PFF-grade prior dominates when it should yield to RAS + coach-archetype when grade < 82 but RAS > 9.5.

WILDCARD coaching-staff-specific QB reaches: Bo Nix (Payton), Penix (Kirk Cousins succession), Jaxson Dart (Daboll). QB reaches by coaching philosophy are happening at a ~5% rate per draft and we have no 'coach-QB-archetype-match' feature above need weighting.

Visit signal is saturated at ~78% — almost every R1 pick visits. We are likely giving too much lift for a visit that essentially every R1 player completes. The discriminating signal is private workout / 'exclusive top-30' not generic visit.

NEED_DRIVEN picks where team reaches 6-12 slots past consensus (Latham, Booker, Grant, Hampton, Jackson, Tyleik Williams) are our second-largest miss cluster. Roster_needs weighting may be too soft when need-value > 8 slots of board value.
recommended_feature_tuning: Add a post-trade cascade step: when a trade fires, re-sample the next 3-5 team boards with BPA-bias shifted toward the prospect pool freshly available (should recover ~2-4 pick slots per draft).
Introduce 'exclusive_visit' flag that distinguishes private workouts / multi-visit prospects from routine top-30s. Down-weight bare visits; up-weight exclusives.
Add coach_qb_archetype_match feature: map each HC/OC to their QB profile (mobile/pocket/play-action) and boost QB prospects who match when team has QB in top-2 needs.
Boost scheme_archetype_tags weight for EDGE/OL prospects when RAS > 9.0 AND PFF < 83 (traits-over-tape cluster). Historically these have gone R1 at 2-3× the board-rank-implied rate.
Add 'reach_tolerance_per_GM' feature — Gutekunst, Beane, Payton, Douglas have documented multi-year patterns of reaching 6-12 slots for trait-based fits. Learn per-GM reach magnitude from 2020-2025.
Soften consensus_rank prior on slots 25-32 by ~15%. Late R1 variance is structurally higher (more trades, more scheme reaches) and our 91% top-32 overlap collapses to ~30% exact-slot in picks 25-32.

methodology_ceiling_analysis.predictable_bucket_ceiling: 27 of 64 picks (42%) are catchable by any well-calibrated board-rank model — ceiling is ~13-14 exact hits per 32-pick R1
methodology_ceiling_analysis.plus_need_driven_ceiling: +12 NEED_DRIVEN picks catchable with sharp roster_needs weighting — ceiling rises to ~19 of 32 (60%)
methodology_ceiling_analysis.plus_scheme_fit_ceiling: +9 SCHEME_FIT picks catchable with coach-archetype-mapping and RAS-override logic — ceiling rises to ~23 of 32 (72%)
methodology_ceiling_analysis.plus_trade_cascade_ceiling: +9 TRADE_CASCADE picks partially catchable (probably ~50% hit rate even with cascade logic) — ceiling rises to ~27 of 32 (84%)
methodology_ceiling_analysis.realistic_perfect_tune_ceiling: 23-25 of 32 exact R1 hits, with the remaining 7-9 picks being a mix of WILDCARD (uncatchable, ~3-4) and residual TRADE/SCHEME noise (~4-5)
methodology_ceiling_analysis.current_gap_to_ceiling: 2026 model hits ~2/32 exact; realistic ceiling is 23-25/32. Biggest single-feature recoverable gains: trade_cascade propagation (+3-4 picks), scheme-RAS override (+2-3 picks), late-R1 variance model (+2-3 picks).
